rationale: Implements a configurable execution timeout for API-launched probes exactly as the PR describes. `src/service/rest_api.rs` adds `probe_execution_timeout: Duration` to `RestApiConfig` with `Default = Duration::from_secs(60)` and a validation branch in `validate_security_defaults` that returns `RestApiValidationError::InvalidOption` when the value is zero, plus a regression test `zero_probe_execution_timeout_is_rejected`. `src/service/rest_server.rs` wraps `execute_probe(...)` in `tokio::time::timeout(probe_timeout, ...)` inside `run_probe_job`, splits the match into `Ok(Ok(result))` / `Ok(Err(error))` / `Err(_elapsed)` arms, and on the elapsed arm sets job status to `Failed` with message `"probe timed out after {}s"` formatted from `probe_timeout.as_secs()`; the existing `drop(permit)` call sits outside the match so the concurrency permit is released. `src/main.rs` adds the `--api-probe-timeout-seconds` clap flag and wires it through `apply_rest_api_cli_overrides`, with a unit test `api_mode_applies_probe_timeout_override` asserting the override is applied. Two end-to-end tests in `tests/rest_server_http_tests.rs` (`probe_execution_timeout_marks_job_failed`, `probe_execution_timeout_releases_concurrency_permit`) spawn the server with 1ms timeout and `max_concurrent_probes: 1`, then assert the second probe is accepted and also times out (not rejected). Docs in `USAGE.md`, `docs/API.md`, and `docs/ROADMAP.md` all match.
